verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Dialogue II.—The Unconfounded.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eranistes and Orthodoxus.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I am come as I promised. ’Tis yours to adopt one of two alternatives, and either furnish a solution of my difficulties, or assent to what I and my friends lay down.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—I accept your challenge, for I think it right and fair.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But we must first recall to mind at what point we left off our discourse yesterday, and what was the conclusion of our argument.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I will remind you of the end.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: I remember our agreeing that the divine Word remained immutable, and took flesh, and was not himself changed into flesh.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—You seem to be content with the points agreed on, for you have faithfully called them to mind.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Yes, and I have already said that the man that withstands teachers so many and so great is indubitably out of his mind.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: I was moreover put to not a little shame to find that Apollinarius used the same terms as the orthodox, although in his books about the incarnation his drift has distinctly been in another direction.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then we affirm that the Divine Word took flesh?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And what do we mean by the flesh?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: A body only, as is the view of Arius and Eunomius, or body and soul?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Body and soul.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—What kind of soul?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: The reasonable soul, or that which is by some termed the phytic, vegetable, that is, vital? for the fable-mongering quackery of the Apollinarians compels us to ask unseemly questions.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Does then Apollinarius make a distinction of souls?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—He says that man is composed of three parts, of a body, a vital soul, and further of a reasonable soul, which he terms mind.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Holy Scripture on the contrary knows only one, not two souls; and this is plainly taught us by the formation of the first man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For it is written God took dust from the earth and “formed man,” and “breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and man became a living soul.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And in the gospels the Lord said to the holy disciples “Fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul; but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And the very divine Moses when he told the tale of them that came down into Egypt and stated with whom each tribal chief had come in, added, “All the souls that came out of Egypt were seventy-five,” reckoning one soul for each immigrant.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And the divine apostle at Troas, when all supposed Eutychus to be dead, said “Trouble not yourselves for his soul is in him.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It is shewn clearly that each man has one soul.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But Apollinarius says two; and that the Divine Word took the unreasonable, and that instead of the reasonable, he was made in the flesh.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: It was on this account that I asked what kind of soul you assert to have been assumed with the body.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I say the reasonable.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For I follow the Divine Scripture.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—We agree then that the “form of a servant” assumed by the Divine Word was complete.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Yes; complete.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And rightly; for since the whole first man became subject to sin, and lost the impression of the Divine Image, and the race followed, it results that the Creator, with the intention of renewing the blurred image, assumed the nature in its entirety, and stamped an imprint far better than the first.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But now I beg you in the first place that the meaning of the terms employed may be made quite clear, that thus our discussion may advance without hindrance, and no investigation of doubtful points intervene to interrupt our conversation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—What you say is admirable.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Ask now concerning whatever point you like.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—What must we call Jesus the Christ?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—By neither name alone, but by both.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For the Divine Man after being made man was named Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: “For,” it is written, “Thou shalt call His name Jesus for he shall save His people from their sins,” and unto you is born this day in the city of David Christ the Lord.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Now these are angels’ voices.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But before the Incarnation he was named God, son of God, only begotten, Lord, Divine Word, and Creator.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For it is written “In the beginning was the Word, and the word was with God, and the word was God,” and “all things were made by Him,” and “He was life,” and “He was the true light which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: There are also other similar passages, declaring the divine nature.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But after the Incarnation He was named Jesus and Christ.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Therefore the Lord Jesus is God only.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—You hear that the divine Word was made man, and do you call him God only?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Since He became man without being changed, but remained just what He was before, we must call Him just what He was.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—The divine Word was and is and will be immutable.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But when He had taken man’s nature He became man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: It behoves us therefore to confess both natures, both that which took, and that which was taken.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—We must name Him by the nobler.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth—Man,—I mean man the animal,—is he a simple or a composite being?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Composed of what component parts?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Of a body and a soul.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And of these natures whether is nobler?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Clearly the soul, for it is reasonable and immortal, and has been entrusted with the sovereignty of the animal.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But the body is mortal and perishable, and without the soul is unreasonable, and a corpse.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then the divine Scripture ought to have called the animal after its more excellent part.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It does so call it, for it calls them that came out of Egypt souls.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For with seventy-five souls, it says, Israel came down into Egypt.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But does the divine Scripture never call any one after the body?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It calls them that are the slaves of flesh, flesh.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For “God,” it is written, “said my spirit shall not always remain in these men, for they are flesh.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But without blame no one is called flesh?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I do not remember.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then I will remind you, and point out to you that even the very saints are called “flesh.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: What would you call the apostles?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Spiritual, or fleshly?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Spiritual;—and leaders and teachers of the spiritual.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Hear now the holy Paul when he says “But when it pleased God who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me by his grace, to reveal his son in me that I might preach him among the heathen, immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood neither went I up to them that were apostles before me.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Does he so style the apostles because he blames them?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Certainly not.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Is it not that he names them after their visible nature, and comparing the calling which is of men with that which is of heaven?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then hear too the psalmist David—“Unto thee shall all flesh come.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Hear too, the prophet Isaiah foretelling “All flesh shall see the salvation of our God.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It is made perfectly plain that Holy Scripture names human nature from the flesh without the least blame.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—I will proceed to give you the yet further proof.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—The fact that sometimes when giving blame the divine Scripture uses only the name of soul.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—And where will you find this in holy Scripture?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Hear the Lord God speaking through the prophet Ezekiel “The soul that sinneth it shall die.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Moreover through the great Moses He saith “If a soul sin—” And again “It shall come to pass that every soul that will not hear that prophet shall be cut off.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And many other passages of the same kind may be found.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—This is plainly proved.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—In cases, then, where there is a certain natural union, and a combination of created things, and of beings connected by service and by time, it is not the custom of holy Scripture to use a name for this being derived only from the nobler nature; it names it indiscriminately both by the meaner and by the nobler.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: If so, how can you find fault with us for calling Christ the Lord, man, after confessing Him to be God, when many things combine to compel us to do so?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—What is there to compel us to call the Saviour Christ, “man”?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—The diverse and mutually inconsistent opinions of the heretics.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—What opinions, and contrary to what?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—That of Arius to that of Sabellius.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: The one divides the substances: the other confounds the hypostases.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Arius introduces three substances, and Sabellius makes one hypostasis instead of three.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Tell me now, how ought we to heal both maladies?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Must we apply the same drug for both ailments, or for each the proper one?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—For each the proper one.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—We shall therefore endeavour to persuade Arius to acknowledge the substance of the Holy Trinity, and we shall adduce proofs of this position from Holy Scripture.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Yes: this ought to be done.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But in arguing with Sabellius we shall adopt the opposite course.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Concerning the substance we shall advance no argument, for even he acknowledges but one.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But we shall do our best to cure the unsound part of his doctrine.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—We say that where he halts is about the hypostases.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Since then he asserts there to be one hypostasis of the Trinity, we shall point out to him that the divine Scripture proclaims three hypostases.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—This is the course to take.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But we have wandered from the subject.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Not at all.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: We are collecting proofs of it, as you will learn in a moment.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But tell me, do you understand that all the heresies which derive their name from Christ, acknowledge both the Godhead of Christ and His manhood?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Do not some acknowledge the godhead alone, and some the manhood alone?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And some but a part of the manhood?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I think so.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But it will be well for us to lay down the names of the holders of these different opinions, that the point under discussion may be made plainer.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—I will tell you the names.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Simon, Menander, Marcion, Valentinus, Basilides, Bardesanes, Cerdo, and Manes, openly denied the humanity of Christ.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: On the other hand Artemon, Theodotus, Sabellius, Paul of Samosata, Marcellus, and Photinus, fell into the diametrically opposite blasphemy; for they preach Christ to be man only, and deny the Godhead which existed before the ages.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Arius and Eunomius make the Godhead of the only begotten a created Godhead, and maintain that He assumed only a body.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Apollinarius confesses that the assumed body was a living body, but in his work deprives the reasonable soul alike of its honour and of its salvation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: This is the contrariety of these corrupt opinions.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But do you, with all due love of truth, tell us, must we institute a discussion with these men, or shall we let them go dashed down headlong and howling to their doom?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It is inhuman to neglect the sick.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Very well; then we must compassionate them, and do our best to heal them.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—If then you had scientifically learned how to cure the body, and round you stood many men asking you to cure them, and shewing their various ailments, such as arise from running at the eyes, injury to the ears, tooth-ache, contraction of the joints, palsy, bile, or phlegm, what would you have done?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Tell me; would you have applied the same treatment to all, or to each that which was appropriate?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I should certainly have given to each the appropriate remedy.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—So by applying cold treatment to the hot, and heating the cold, and loosing the strained, and giving tension to the loose, and drying the moist, and moistening the dry, you would have driven out the diseases and restored the health which they had expelled.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—This is the treatment prescribed by medical science, for contraries, it is said, are the remedies of contraries.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—If you were a gardener, would you give the same treatment to all plants? or their own to the mulberry and the fig, and so to the pear, to the apple, and to the vine what is fitting to each, and in a word to each plant its own proper culture?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It is obvious that each plant requires its own treatment.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And if you undertook to be a ship builder, and saw that the mast wanted repair, would you try to mend it in the same way as you would the tiller? or would you give it the proper treatment of a mast?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—There is no question about these things: everything demands its own treatment, be it plant or limb or gear or tackle.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then is it not monstrous to apply to the body and to things without life to each its own appropriate treatment, and not to keep this rule of treatment in the case of the soul?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Most unjust; nay, rather stupid than unrighteous.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: They who adopt any other method are quite unskilled in the healing art.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then in disputing against each heresy we shall use the appropriate remedy?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And it is fitting treatment to add what is wanting and to remove what is superfluous?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—In endeavouring then to cure Photinus and Marcellus and their adherents, in order to carry out the rule of treatment, what should we add?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—The acknowledgment of the Godhead of Christ, for it is this that they lack.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But about the manhood we will say nothing to them, for they acknowledge the Lord Christ to be man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—You are right.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And in arguing with Arius and Eunomius about the incarnation of the only begotten, what should we persuade them to add to their own confession?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—The assumption of the soul; for they say that the divine Word took only a body.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And what does Apollinarius lack to make his teaching accurate about the incarnation?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Not to separate the mind from the soul, but to confess that, with the body, was assumed a reasonable soul.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then shall we dispute with him on this point?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But under this head what did we assert to be confessed, and what altogether denied, by Marcion, Valentinus, Manes and their adherents?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—That they admitted their belief in the Godhead of Christ, but do not accept the doctrine of His manhood.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—We shall therefore do our best to persuade them to accept also the doctrine of the manhood, and not to call the divine incarnation a mere appearance.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It will be well so to do.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—We will therefore tell them that it is right to style the Christ not only God, but also man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And how is it possible for us to induce others to style the Christ ‘man’ while we excuse ourselves from doing so?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: They will not yield to our persuasion, but on the contrary will convict us of agreeing with them.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—And how can we, confessing as we do that the divine Word took flesh and a reasonable soul, agree with them?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—If we confess the fact, why then shun the word?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It is right to name the Christ from His nobler qualities.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Keep this rule then.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Do not speak of Him as crucified, nor yet as risen from the dead, and so on.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—But these are the names of the sufferings of salvation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Denial of the sufferings implies denial of the salvation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And the name Man is the name of a nature.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Not to pronounce the name is to deny the nature: denial of the nature is denial of the sufferings, and denial of the sufferings does away with the salvation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I hold it profitable to acknowledge the assumed nature; but to style the Saviour of the world man is to belittle the glory of the Lord.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Do you then deem yourself wiser than Peter and Paul; aye, and than the Saviour Himself?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For the Lord said to the Jews “Why do ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I heard of my Father?”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And He frequently called Himself Son of Man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And the meritorious Peter, in his sermon to the Jewish people, says,—“Ye men of Israel, hear these words.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Jesus of Nazareth, a man approved of God among you.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And the blessed Paul, when bringing the message of salvation to the chiefs of the Areopagus, among many other things said this,—

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: “And the times of this ignorance God winked at; but now commandeth all men everywhere to repent: Because he hath appointed a day in the which he will judge the world in righteousness by that man whom he hath ordained, whereof he hath given assurance unto all men, in that he hath raised him from the dead.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He then who excuses himself from using the name appointed and preached by the Lord and his Apostles deems himself wiser than even these great instructors, aye, even than the very well-spring of the wisest.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—They gave this instruction to the unbelievers.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Now the greater part of the world has professed the faith.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But we have still among us Jews and pagans and of heretics systems innumerable, and to each of these we must give fit and appropriate teaching.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But, supposing we were all of one mind, tell me now, what harm is there in calling the Christ both God and man?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Do we not behold in Him perfect Godhead, and manhood likewise lacking in nothing?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—This we have owned again and again.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Why then deny what we have again and again owned?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I hold it unnecessary to call the Christ ‘man,’—especially when believer is conversing with believer.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Do you consider the divine Apostle a believer?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Yes: a teacher of all believers.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And do you deem Timothy worthy of being so styled?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Yes: both as a disciple of the Apostle, and as a teacher of the rest.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Very well: then hear the teacher of teachers writing to his very perfect disciple.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: “There is one God, and one mediator between God and man, the man Christ Jesus, who gave himself a ransom for all.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Do stop your idle prating, and laying down the law about divine names.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Moreover in this passage that very name ‘mediator’ stands indicative both of Godhead and of manhood.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He is called a mediator because He does not exist as God alone; for how, if He had had nothing of our nature could He have mediated between us and God?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But since as God He is joined with God as having the same substance, and as man with us, because from us He took the form of a servant, He is properly termed a mediator, uniting in Himself distinct qualities by the unity of natures of Godhead, I mean, and of manhood.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—But was not Moses called a mediator, though only a man?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—He was a type of the reality: but the type has not all the qualities of the reality.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Wherefore though Moses was not by nature God, yet, to fulfil the type, he was called a god.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For He says “See, I have made thee a god to Pharaoh.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And then directly afterwards he assigns him also a Prophet as though to God, for “Aaron thy brother,” He says, “shall be thy Prophet.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But the reality is by nature God, and by nature man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—But who would call one not having the distinct characteristics of the archetype, a type?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—The imperial images, it seems, you do not call images of the emperor.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Yet they have not all the characteristics which their archetype has.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For in the first place they have neither life nor reason; secondly they have no inner organs, heart, I mean, and belly and liver and the adjacent parts.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Further they present the appearance of the organs of sense, but perform none of their functions, for they neither hear, nor speak, nor see; they cannot write; they cannot walk, nor perform any other human action; and yet they are called imperial statues.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: In this sense Moses was a mediator and Christ was a mediator; but the former as an image and type and the latter as reality.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But that I may make this point clearer to you from yet another authority, call to mind the words used of Melchisedec in the Epistle to the Hebrews.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Those in which the divine Apostle comparing the Levitical priesthood with that of the Christ likens Melchisedec in other respects to the Lord Christ, and says that the Lord had the priesthood after the order of Melchisedec.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I think the words of the divine Apostle are as follows;—“For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the most high God who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him; to whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all; first being by interpretation king of righteousness, and after that also king of Salem, which is king of peace; without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the son of God; abideth a priest continually.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: I presume you spoke of this passage.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Yes, I spoke of this; and I must praise you for not mutilating it, but for quoting the whole.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Tell me now, does each one of these points fit Melchisedec in nature and reality?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Who has the audacity to deny a fitness where the divine apostle has asserted it?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then you say that all this fits Melchisedec by nature?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Do you say that he was a man, or assumed some other nature?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Begotten or unbegotten?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—You are asking very absurd questions.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—The fault lies with you for openly opposing the truth.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Answer then.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—There is one only unbegotten, who is God and Father.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then we assert that Melchisedec was begotten?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But the passage about him teaches the opposite.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Remember the words which you quoted a moment ago, “Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days nor end of life.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: How then do the words “Without father and without mother” fit him; and how the statement that he neither received beginning of existence nor end, since all this transcends humanity?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—These things do in fact overstep the limits of human nature.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then shall we say that the Apostle told lies?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—How then is it possible both to testify to the truth of the Apostle, and apply the supernatural to Melchisedec?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—The passage is a very difficult one, and requires much explanation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—For any one willing to consider it with attention it will not be hard to attain perception of the meaning of the words.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: After saying “without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days nor end of life,” the divine Apostle adds “made like unto the Son of God, abideth a priest continually.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Here he plainly teaches us that the Lord Christ is archetype of Melchisedec in things concerning the human nature.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And he speaks of Melchisedec as “made like unto the Son of God.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Now let us examine the point in this manner;—do you say that the Lord had a father according to the flesh?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Certainly not.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—He was born of the holy Virgin alone.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—He is therefore properly styled “without father”?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Do you say that according to the divine Nature He had a mother?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Certainly not.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—For He was begotten of the Father alone before the ages?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And yet, as the generation He has of the Father is ineffable, He is spoken of as “without descent.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: “Who” says the prophet “shall declare His generation?”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—You are right.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Thus it becomes Him to have neither beginning of days nor end of life; for He is without beginning, indestructible, and, in a word, eternal, and coeternal with the Father.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—This is my view too.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But we must now consider how this fits the admirable Melchisedec.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—As an image and type.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: The image, as we have just observed, has not all the properties of the archetype.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Thus to the Saviour these qualities are proper both by nature and in reality; but the story of the origin of the race has attributed them to Melchisedec.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For after telling us of the father of the patriarch Abraham, and of the father and mother of Isaac, and in like manner of Jacob and of his sons, and exhibiting the pedigree of our first forefathers, of Melchisedec it records neither the father nor the mother, nor does it teach that he traced his descent from any one of Noah’s sons, to the end that he may be a type of Him who is in reality without father, and without mother.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And this is what the divine Apostle would have us understand, for in this very passage he says further, “But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Then, since Holy Scripture has not mentioned his parents, can he be called without father and without mother?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—If he had really been without father and without mother, he would not have been an image, but a reality.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But since these are his qualities not by nature, but according to the dispensation of the Divine Scripture, he exhibits the type of the reality.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—The type must have the character of the archetype.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Is man called an image of God?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Man is not an image of God, but was made in the image of God.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Listen then to the Apostle.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He says: “For a man indeed ought not to cover his head, forasmuch as he is the image and glory of God.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Granted, then, that he is an image of God.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—According to your argument then he must needs have plainly preserved the characters of the archetype, and have been uncreate, uncompounded, and infinite.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He ought in like manner to have been able to create out of the non existent, he ought to have fashioned all things by his word and without labour, in addition to this to have been free from sickness, sorrow, anger, and sin, to have been immortal and incorruptible and to possess all the qualities of the archetype.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Man is not an image of God in every respect.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Though truly an image in the qualities in which you would grant him to be so, you will find that he is separated by a wide interval from the reality.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Consider now too this point.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: The divine Apostle calls the Son the image of the Father; for he says “Who is the image of the invisible God?”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—What then; has not the Son all the qualities of the Father?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—He is not Father.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He is not uncaused.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He is not unbegotten.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—If He were He would not be Son.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then does not what I said hold good; the image has not all the qualities of the archetype?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Thus too the divine Apostle said that Melchisedec is made like unto the Son of God.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Suppose we grant that he is without Father and without Mother and without descent, as you have said.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But how are we to understand his having neither beginning of days nor end of life?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—The holy Moses when writing the ancient genealogy tells us how Adam being so many years old begat Seth, and when he had lived so many years he ended his life.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: So too he writes of Seth, of Enoch, and of the rest, but of Melchisedec he mentions neither beginning of existence nor end of life.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Thus as far as the story goes he has neither beginning of days nor end of life, but in truth and reality the only begotten Son of God never began to exist and shall never have an end.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then, so far as what belongs to God and is really divine is concerned, Melchisedec is a type of the Lord Christ; but as far as the priesthood is concerned, which belongs rather to man than to God, the Lord Christ was made a priest after the order of Melchisedec.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For Melchisedec was a high priest of the people, and the Lord Christ for all men has made the right holy offering of salvation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—We have spent many words on this matter.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Yet more were needed, as you know, for you said the point was a difficult one.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Let us return to the question before us.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—What was the question?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—On my remarking that Christ must not be called man, but only God, you yourself besides many other testimonies adduced also the well known words of the Apostle which he has used in his epistle Timothy—“One God, one mediator between God and men, the man, Christ Jesus, who gave himself a ransom for all to be testified in due time.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—I remember from what point we diverged into this digression.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: It was when I had said that the name of mediator exhibits the two natures of the Saviour, and you said that Moses was called a mediator though he was only a man and not God and man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: I was therefore under the necessity of following up these points to show that the type has not all the qualities of the archetype.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Tell me, then, whether you allow that the Saviour ought also to be called man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I call Him God, for He is God’s Son.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—If you call him God, because you have learnt that he is God’s Son, call him also man, for he often called Himself “Son of Man.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—The name man does not apply to Him in the same way as the name God.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—As not really belonging to Him or for some other reason?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—God is his name by nature; man is the designation of the Incarnation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But are we to look on the Incarnation as real, or as something imaginary and false?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—If then the grace of the Incarnation is real, and what we call Incarnation is the divine Word’s being made man, then the name man is real; for after taking man’s nature He is called man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Before His passion He was styled man, but afterward He was no longer so styled.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But it was after the Passion and the Resurrection that the divine Apostle wrote the Epistle to Timothy wherein he speaks of the Saviour Christ as man, and writing after the Passion and the Resurrection to the Corinthians he exclaims “For since by man came death, by man came also the resurrection of the dead.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And in order to make his meaning clear he adds, “For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And after the Passion and the Resurrection the divine Peter, in his address to the Jews, called Him man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And after His being taken up into heaven, Stephen the victorious, amid the storm of stones, said to the Jews, “Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Are we to suppose ourselves wiser than the illustrious heralds of the truth?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I do not suppose myself wiser than the holy doctors, but I fail to find the use of the name.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—How then could you persuade them that deny the incarnation of the Lord, Marcionists, I mean, and Manichees, and all the rest who are thus unsound, to accept the teaching of the truth, unless you adduce these and similar proofs with the object of shewing that the Lord Christ is not God only but also man?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Perhaps it is necessary to adduce them.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Why not then teach the faithful the reality of the doctrine?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Are you forgetful of the apostolic precept enjoining us to be “ready to give an answer.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Now let us look at the matter in this light.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Does the best general engage the enemy, attack with arrows and javelins, and endeavour to break their column all alone, or does he also arm his men, and marshal them, and rouse their hearts to play the man?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—He ought rather to do this latter.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Yes; for it is not the part of a general to expose his own life, and take his place in the ranks, and let his men go fast asleep, but rather to keep them awake for their work at their post.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—This is what the divine Paul did, for in writing to them who had made profession of their faith he said, “Take unto you the whole armour of God that ye be able to stand against the wiles of the Devil.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And again, “Stand therefore with your loins girt about with truth,” and so on.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Bear in mind too what we have already said, that a physician supplies what nature lacks.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Does he find the cold redundant?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He supplies the hot, and so on with the rest; and this is what the Lord does.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—And where will you show that the Lord has done this?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—In the holy gospels.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Show me then and fulfil your promise.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—What did the Jews consider our Saviour Christ?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And that He was also God they were wholly ignorant.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Was it not then necessary for the ignorant to learn?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Listen to Him then saying to them: “Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of these works do ye stone me?”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And when they replied: “For a good work we stone thee not, but for blasphemy, and because that thou being a man makest thyself God,” He added “It is written in your law I said ye are gods.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: If he called them gods unto whom the word of God came and the scripture cannot be broken, say ye of Him whom the Father hath sanctified and sent into the world thou blasphemest, because I said I am the Son of God?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: If I do not the works of my father believe me not…that I am in the Father and the Father is in me.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—In the passages you have just read you have shewn that the Lord shewed Himself to the Jews to be God and not man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Yes, for they did not need to learn what they knew; that He was a man they knew, but they did not know that He was from the beginning God.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He adopted this same course in the case of the Pharisees; for when He saw them accosting Him as a mere man He asked them “What think ye of Christ?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Whose son is He?”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And when they said “Of David” He went on “How then doth David calling him Lord say ‘The Lord said unto my Lord sit thou on my right hand.’”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Then He goes on to argue, “If then He is His Lord how is He His Son?”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—You have brought testimony against yourself, for the Lord plainly taught the Pharisees to call Him not “Son of David” but “Lord of David.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Wherefore He is distinctly shown wishing to be called God and not man.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—I am afraid you have not attended to the divine teaching.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He did not repudiate the name of “Son of David,” but He added that He ought also to be believed to be Lord of David.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: This He clearly shews in the words “If He is his Lord how is He then his Son?”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: He did not say “if He is Lord He is not Son,” but “how is He his Son?” instead of saying in one respect He is Lord and in another Son.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: These passages both distinctly show the Godhead and the manhood.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—There is no need of argument.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: The Lord distinctly teaches that He does not wish to be called Son of David.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Then He ought to have told the blind men and the woman of Canaan and the multitude not to call Him Son of David, and yet the blind men cried out “Thou Son of David have mercy on us.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And the woman of Canaan “Have mercy on me O Son of David; my daughter is grievously vexed with a Devil.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And the multitude: “Hosanna to the Son of David!

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And not only did He not take it ill, but even praised their faith; for the blind He freed from their long weary night and granted them the power of sight; the maddened and distraught daughter of the woman of Canaan He healed and drove out the wicked demon; and when the chief priests and Pharisees were offended at them that shouted “Hosanna to the Son of David” He did not merely not prevent them from shouting, but even sanctioned their acclamation, for, said He, “I tell you that if these should hold their peace the stones would immediately cry out.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—He put up with this style of address before the resurrection in condescension to the weakness of them that had not yet properly believed.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But after the resurrection these names are needless.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Where shall we rank the blessed Paul? among the perfect or the imperfect?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—It is wrong to joke about serious things.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—It is wrong to make light of the reading of the divine oracles.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—And who is such a wretch as to despise his own salvation?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Answer my question, and then you will learn your ignorance.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—What question?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Where are we to rank the divine Apostle?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Plainly among the most perfect, and one of the perfect teachers.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And when did he begin his teaching?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—After the ascension of the Saviour, the coming of the Spirit, and the stoning of the victorious Stephen.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Paul, at the very end of his life, when writing his last letter to his disciple Timothy, and in giving him, as it were, his paternal inheritance by will, added “Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my gospel.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Then he went on to mention his sufferings on behalf of the gospel, and thus showed its truth saying, “Wherein I suffer trouble as an evil doer even unto bonds.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: It were easy for me to adduce many similar testimonies, but I have judged it needless to do so.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—You promised to prove that the Lord supplied the lacking instruction to them that needed, and you have shown that He discoursed about His own Godhead to the Pharisees, and to the rest of the Jews.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But that He gave also His instruction about the flesh you have not shewn.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—It would have been quite superfluous to have discoursed about the flesh which was before their eyes, for He was plainly seen eating and drinking and toiling and sleeping.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Furthermore, to omit the many and various events before the passion, after His resurrection He proved to His disbelieving disciples not His Godhead but His manhood; for He said, “Behold my hands and my feet that it is I myself.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Handle me and see for a spirit hath not flesh and bones as ye see me have.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Now I have fulfilled my promise, for we have proved the giving of instruction about the Godhead to them that were ignorant of the Godhead, and about the resurrection of the flesh to them that denied this latter.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Cease therefore from contending, and confess the two natures of the Saviour.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—There were two before the union, but, after combining, they made one nature.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—When do you say that the union was effected?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I say at the exact moment of the conception.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And do you deny that the divine Word existed before the conception?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I say that He was before the ages.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—And that the flesh was co-existent with Him?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But was formed, after the salutation of the angel, of the Holy Ghost?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Therefore before the union there were not two natures but only one.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For if the Godhead pre-existed, but the manhood was not co-existent, being formed after the angelic salutation, and the union being coincident with the formation, then before the union there was one nature, that which exists always and existed before the ages.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Now let us again consider this point.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Do you understand the making of flesh or becoming man to be anything other than the union?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
